---
title: "Professor Claude"
subtitle: "A personalized course factory for the math in hard papers"
toc: false
---

This is a personalized course factory. Point it at an academic paper whose math
methods you cannot follow, and it builds an applied math and statistics course
tailored to your background and how you learn, one that re-derives and re-codes the
method from the ground up. You learn the method by rebuilding it, not by reading
someone else's summary.

Each course is assembled from small modules. Shared prerequisites live in a
recyclable foundations library, so a building block like "the derivative as a
slope" is written once, linked wherever it is needed, and never re-taught from
scratch.

The whole thing is one Quarto site that works on a phone, with code and plots you
can run and edit inline. You practice the code anywhere instead of only reading
about it.

This published site is just an example, tuned to one learner (a strong R programmer
with rusty AP-level calculus). The system is meant to be forked and pointed at your
own background, so the courses here are demos, not the product.

## How to use this site

Pick a course from the sidebar. Each one opens with a syllabus and a roadmap, then
runs module by module up to a capstone. The roadmap page is where a course points
to its prerequisites: foundation building blocks (vectors, derivatives, likelihood,
and so on) or earlier courses it assumes you have seen.

Cells marked live run in your browser. Edit them, hit Run, and watch the output
change; nothing to set up. Lessons follow a fixed order (intuition, worked example,
notation, derivation, practice, self-check), so reading top to bottom is the
intended path.

## Feedback welcome

This is an open project on
[GitHub](https://github.com/radlinsky/professor-claude#readme), and issues,
corrections, and suggestions are genuinely welcome. [Open one on the issues
page](https://github.com/radlinsky/professor-claude/issues).
